steps.title: Distribute and combine like terms
steps.content: Drop the parentheses and combine like terms:$$=2x^2+x-9+x^2+6x+1$$$$=3x^2+7x-8$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The simplified expression is $3x^2+7x-8$. That matches choice D — D is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice D is correct. The given expression is equivalent to $\left(2{x}^{2}+x+\left(-9\right)\right)+\left({x}^{2}+6x+1\right)$, which can be rewritten as $\left(2{x}^{2}+{x}^{2}\right)+\left(x+6x\right)+\left(-9+1\right)$. Adding like terms in this expression yields $3{x}^{2}+7x+\left(-8\right)$, or $3{x}^{2}+7x-8$.
